rag_info.chunk_content: Historically, Lotus Technology conducted its value-added telecommunication services operation in the Chinese mainland through the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and the VIE’s subsidiaries, with which the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), Lotus Technology’s wholly owned subsidiary, the VIE, and the VIE’s nominee equity holders entered into certain contractual arrangements (“VIE Arrangements”). Since early 2023, Lotus Technology Group has implemented a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations (the “Restructuring”). In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the VIE, and nominee equity holders of the VIE entered into a series of agreements (“VIE Restructuring Agreements”), pursuant to which, i) the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. (“Sanya Lotus”) and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd. (“Hangzhou Lotus”), subsidiaries of the VIE, in February 2023; ii) all the VIE’s assets and liabilities (excluding the Internet Content Provider (ICP) license), business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees were transferred to a subsidiary of the WFOE at nil consideration; iii) the VIE Arrangements were terminated on June 30, 2023. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement, Lotus Technology's Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) has agreed to provide to the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) with comprehensive consulting services and other services, including but not limited to licensing to VIE intellectual property rights legally owned by WFOE; development, installation, maintenance, and update of website, apps, network, and system involved in VIE’s business; providing VIE with software and software technology and solutions; technical support and training for employees of VIE; assisting VIE in market research, business planning, and strategies; providing marketing and promotion services, customer management, finance management, and other related services. VIE and VIE’s subsidiaries (together as VIEs) shall pay WFOE service fees at the amount of 100% of the total consolidated profit of VIEs, after deduction of any accumulated deficit of the VIEs in respect of the preceding financial year(s), necessary operating costs, expenses, and taxes. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the WFOE may adjust the amount of the service fee according to services provided by WFOE to VIEs, the VIE’s operational conditions, and development needs. The WFOE shall calculate the service fee on a quarterly basis and issue a corresponding invoice to the VIEs. The VIEs must make the payment to the WFOE within ten business days of receiving such invoice.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement 
summary: The Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement establishes a comprehensive partnership between WFOE and VIE, where WFOE will deliver a variety of consulting and support services, including intellectual property licensing, digital platform development, software solutions, technical support, employee training, market research, business planning, and marketing services. VIE and its subsidiaries are obligated to pay WFOE service fees that amount to 100% of their total consolidated profits, adjusted for any previous deficits and necessary expenses, with payments due within ten business days of invoicing. WFOE has the authority to modify these fees based on the services provided and VIE's operational requirements. The agreement restricts VIE from seeking similar third-party services without WFOE's consent and stipulates that any intellectual property created by VIE during the agreement will be owned by WFOE. This agreement is designed to be indefinite, with VIE unable to terminate it for any reason throughout its duration.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The nominee equity holders of the VIE did not participate in income and loss and did not have the power to direct the activities of the VIE that most significantly impact the VIE’s economic performance. Accordingly, the VIE was considered a variable interest entity. Because Lotus Technology's wholly owned subsidiary, WFOE, has (i) the power to direct the activities of Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd. (the VIE) that most significantly impact the economic performance of the VIE; and (ii) the right to receive benefits from the VIE that could potentially be significant to the VIE, WFOE is the primary beneficiary of the VIE. Under the terms of the VIE Arrangements, Lotus Technology's wholly owned subsidiary, WFOE, has (i) the right to receive economic benefits that could potentially be significant to Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd. (the VIE) in the form of service fees under the Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement; (ii) the right to unconditionally receive all dividends or interest declared by the VIE and all of the assets of the VIE; (iii) the right to receive the benefits of the VIE through its exclusive option to acquire 100% of the equity interests in the VIE, to the extent permitted under respective laws and regulations. Accordingly, the financial statements of the VIE are included in Lotus Technology's consolidated and combined financial statements.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (e) VIE 
summary: Prior to the restructuring outlined in note 1(d), the Company's subsidiary, Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), established contractual agreements with Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd. (the Variable Interest Entity, or VIE) and its shareholders, enabling the Company to exert control over the VIE and its subsidiaries, collectively referred to as the "VIEs." The VIEs primarily engage in providing value-added telecommunication services. The equity interests in the VIE are held by nominee equity holders—Mr. Li Shufu, Mr. Feng Qingfeng, Mr. Li Donghui, and Mr. Liu Bin—who act on behalf of the WFOE. 

On August 9, 2021, a contractual agreement was formed among the WFOE, the VIE, and the nominee holders, which was later replaced on March 8, 2022, by a series of agreements including an Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement, an Exclusive Purchase Option Agreement, and others, collectively known as the VIE Arrangements. These arrangements effectively transfer all legal rights, including voting and disposition rights, from the nominee holders to the WFOE, which does not share in the VIE's income or losses and lacks authority over its key operational activities. Consequently, the VIE is classified as a variable interest entity, with the WFOE recognized as its primary beneficiary due to its significant control over the VIE's economic performance and rights to substantial benefits.

The VIE Arrangements grant the WFOE critical rights, such as receiving service fees, accessing declared dividends and assets, and an exclusive option to acquire the VIE's equity interests, subject to legal constraints. As a result, the financial results of the VIE are consolidated into the Company's financial statements. The arrangements clarify that the nominee equity holders do not have rights to the VIE's net assets or responsibilities for any deficits, which are instead assumed by the Company through the WFOE, thus attributing all net liabilities and losses of the VIEs to the Company.

title: (d)  VIE Reorganization 
summary: In early 2023, the Company commenced a significant Restructuring process concerning its value-added telecommunication services in mainland China, which had historically been operated through a Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and its subsidiaries. This process involved the execution of VIE Restructuring Agreements, aimed at streamlining operations and consolidating ownership.

Key actions taken during this Restructuring included:

1. In February 2023, the Company's wholly owned subsidiary, WFOE, acquired 100% equity interests in two subsidiaries of the VIE: Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd.
2. The transfer of all VIE assets and liabilities—excluding the ICP license—along with business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees to a subsidiary of WFOE occurred at no cost.
3. The VIE Arrangements were officially terminated on June 30, 2023.

The Group has assessed that this restructuring does not represent a strategic shift and will not have a significant impact on its operations or financial results. This transition reflects a move towards a more direct ownership structure while maintaining compliance with relevant regulatory and legal frameworks in the telecommunications sector.

title: (e)  VIE 
summary: In the context of the VIE (Variable Interest Entity) structure, prior to the Restructuring detailed in note 1(d), the Company's wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) established contractual arrangements with Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd. ("VIE") and its shareholders. This arrangement enabled the Company to exert control over the operations of the VIE and its subsidiaries, collectively referred to as the "VIEs," which primarily offer value-added telecommunication services. The equity interests of the VIE are held by nominee equity holders: Mr. Li Shufu, Mr. Feng Qingfeng, Mr. Li Donghui, and Mr. Liu Bin, who represent the WFOE.

On August 9, 2021, WFOE, the VIE, and the nominee equity holders entered into an initial contractual agreement. This agreement was subsequently replaced by a more comprehensive series of contractual arrangements on March 8, 2022. These arrangements consist of five key components: (i) an Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement, (ii) an Exclusive Purchase Option Agreement, (iii) an Equity Pledge Agreement, (iv) Powers of Attorney, and (v) Spousal Consent Letters. These contracts are designed to ensure that the WFOE maintains effective control over the VIE and its operations, thereby facilitating compliance with regulatory requirements while allowing the Company to benefit from the VIE's business activities.

summary: The Group has secured licenses to operate in the lifestyle vehicle sector (excluding sports cars) and issued 216,700,000 ordinary shares in exchange for these trademark licenses. Following a Reorganization on June 24, 2022, which transferred the Lotus BEV business to the Group, Mr. Shufu Li retained control over both entities, classifying the transaction as a common control event. The financial statements reflect the consolidated results of the Lotus BEV business, assuming the corporate structure was in place for the relevant periods.

On March 15, 2022, the Company executed a 10-for-1 stock split, increasing the total number of ordinary shares to 2,167,000,000 and adjusting the par value accordingly. The Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements are incorporated into the consolidated financial statements, with the Company assuming rights and obligations from the VIEs. However, regulatory challenges pose risks, including potential fines, restructuring requirements, and restrictions on overseas proceeds, which could impact the Company's ability to consolidate VIE results.

As of December 31, 2022, the Group reported significant increases in both assets and liabilities of its VIEs, with total assets rising to US$186,691 and liabilities reaching US$208,927. The VIEs reported no revenue but a net loss of US$52,431, highlighting financial challenges. The Group's consolidated financial position reflects accumulated losses and a need for additional liquidity to sustain operations.

The Company is exploring various strategies for revenue growth and cost management, aiming to enhance operational efficiency. However, the feasibility of these plans is contingent on external factors. The Group's financial statements do not reflect necessary adjustments if the Company cannot continue as a going concern.

The summary also details the Group's engagement with government grants, share options, and related party transactions, emphasizing the importance of these elements in the overall financial landscape. The Parent Company Condensed Balance Sheets and Statements of Comprehensive Loss illustrate significant losses and a deteriorating financial position, with total liabilities increasing to US$2,004,950 by June 30, 2023.

Overall, while the Group has made strides in asset management and operational strategies, it faces substantial financial challenges, regulatory risks, and the need for effective liquidity management to navigate future uncertainties.

title: Our Holding Company Structure and China Operations 
summary: The annual report provides a detailed overview of Lotus Technology Inc.'s corporate structure, emphasizing its role as a holding company registered in the Cayman Islands that operates through subsidiaries in China and Europe. Historically, Lotus managed its business through a Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and contractual agreements involving a Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) and nominee shareholders. However, following a restructuring in early 2023, Lotus has transitioned to conducting its operations in mainland China solely through its subsidiaries, having acquired full equity interests in two former VIE subsidiaries and terminated the VIE Agreements. This restructuring is not expected to materially impact the company's operations or financial results.

Despite this organizational change, Lotus faces significant risks associated with its operations in mainland China, including navigating complex regulatory landscapes, obtaining necessary approvals for offshore offerings, and dealing with antimonopoly actions and cybersecurity regulations. These challenges pose potential threats to the company's ability to attract foreign investments and list on U.S. stock exchanges, which could adversely affect the value of its securities. The Chinese government's extensive regulatory authority further complicates matters, as increased oversight could restrict Lotus's ability to offer securities and lead to a decline in their value. Additionally, the legal environment in mainland China presents uncertainties that could negatively impact the company's operations. For a more detailed examination of these risks, the report refers to specific sections addressing the complexities and potential adverse effects of doing business in China.

title: (b) Summary financial information of the Group’s VIEs in the unaudited condensed consolidated and combined financial statements 
summary: The Company has historically provided value-added telecommunication services in mainland China through a Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and its subsidiaries, utilizing contractual arrangements with Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. (WFOE), its wholly owned subsidiary. In early 2023, the Company restructured its operations, leading to WFOE acquiring 100% equity interests in two VIE subsidiaries and transferring all VIE assets and liabilities, except for its ICP license, to a WFOE subsidiary at no cost. The VIE Arrangements were officially terminated on June 30, 2023, after which the Group ceased consolidating the VIE.

The restructuring did not signify a strategic shift and is not expected to significantly impact the Group's operations or financial results. The unaudited financial statements for the six months ending June 30, 2023, reflect the VIE's revenues, net loss, and cash flow information, with all intercompany transactions prior to the restructuring eliminated from consolidation.

Key financial metrics for the VIEs during the six-month periods ending June 30, 2023, and 2022 reveal several important trends: 

1. **Revenues**: Both periods reported no revenues.
2. **Net Loss**: The net loss increased significantly to US$74,137 in 2023 from US$8,095 in 2022, largely due to restructuring expenses.
3. **Operating Activities**: Cash used in operating activities rose to US$8,281 in 2023 from US$5,599 in 2022, indicating higher operational outflows.
4. **Investing Activities**: Cash used in investing activities decreased to US$38,400 in 2023 from US$61,164 in 2022, reflecting reduced expenditures.
5. **Financing Activities**: There was a notable shift to a net outflow of US$107,317 in 2023, compared to an inflow of US$64,487 in 2022, highlighting significant financing outflows during deconsolidation.
6. **Exchange Rate Impact**: Currency fluctuations led to a cash outflow of US$2,573 in 2023, slightly lower than US$3,022 in 2022.
7. **Net Cash Decrease**: The net cash decrease was US$156,571 in 2023, a stark contrast to the US$5,298 decrease in 2022.
8. **Cash Position**: Cash at the beginning of 2023 was US$156,571, but by the end of the period, it was depleted to US$0, compared to US$43,796 at the end of 2022.

The financial data underscores the challenges faced by the VIEs during the restructuring, with increased net losses and cash outflows across various activities. Additionally, significant intercompany transactions were noted, which were eliminated in the consolidation process, further highlighting the complexities of the Group's financial restructuring.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The nominee equity holders of the VIE did not participate in income and loss and did not have the power to direct the activities of the VIE that most significantly impact the VIE’s economic performance. Accordingly, the VIE was considered a variable interest entity. Because Lotus Technology's wholly owned subsidiary, WFOE, has (i) the power to direct the activities of the VIE that most significantly impact the economic performance of the VIE; and (ii) the right to receive benefits from the VIE that could potentially be significant to the VIE, WFOE is the primary beneficiary of the VIE. Under the terms of the VIE Arrangements, Lotus Technology, through its wholly owned subsidiary, WFOE, has (i) the right to receive economic benefits that could potentially be significant to the VIE in the form of service fees under the Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement; (ii) the right to unconditionally receive all dividends or interest declared by the VIE and all of the assets of the VIE; (iii) the right to receive the benefits of the VIE through its exclusive option to acquire 100% of the equity interests in the VIE, to the extent permitted under respective laws and regulations. Accordingly, the financial statements of the VIE are included in Lotus Technology’s consolidated and combined financial statements. Under the terms of the VIE Arrangements, the VIE’s nominee equity holders have no rights to the net assets nor have the obligations to fund the deficit, and such rights and obligations have been vested to Lotus Technology through WFOE.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (e)    VIE 
summary: Prior to the restructuring mentioned in note 1(d), the Company's subsidiary, WFOE, established contractual agreements with Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd. (the "VIE") and its shareholders, allowing the Company to control the operations of the VIE and its subsidiaries, collectively referred to as the "VIEs." The VIEs primarily provide value-added telecommunication services, with equity interests held by nominee shareholders who do not participate in the VIE's income or losses and lack authority over its key activities. 

On August 9, 2021, a contractual agreement was formed between the WFOE, VIE, and the nominee equity holders, which was later replaced on March 8, 2022, by a series of agreements known as the VIE Arrangements. These arrangements include an Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement, Exclusive Purchase Option Agreement, Equity Pledge Agreement, Powers of Attorney, and Spousal Consent Letters. Through these agreements, the WFOE gains significant control over the VIE, directing its key activities and receiving substantial economic benefits, thus being identified as the primary beneficiary.

The VIE Arrangements grant the Company, via the WFOE, rights to receive service fees, access to declared dividends and assets, and an exclusive option to acquire 100% of the VIE's equity interests, ensuring that the VIE's financial statements are consolidated into the Company's financial reports. The nominee equity holders do not hold rights to the VIE's net assets or bear obligations for its deficits, which are instead attributed to the Company through the WFOE.

The principal terms of the VIE Arrangements outline the ownership structure, control rights, financial arrangements, operational responsibilities, duration and termination conditions, and regulatory compliance requirements, all of which are essential for understanding the legal and financial implications for the parties involved.

title: UNAUDITED PRO FORMA CONDENSED COMBINED FINANCIAL INFORMATION 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Unaudited Pro Forma Condensed Combined Financial Information**

The unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information presented herein is derived from the balance sheets and statements of operations of LTC and LCAA, aimed at evaluating the financial implications of their Business Combination and related financing transactions, collectively referred to as the “Transactions.” 

LCAA, which was incorporated on January 5, 2021, successfully completed its Initial Public Offering (IPO) on March 15, 2021, raising gross proceeds of US$250 million through the issuance of 25 million Units at a price of US$10.00 each. As of December 31, 2023, LCAA reported US$234.734 million remaining in its Trust Account. In conjunction with the Business Combination, holders of 20,518,519 LCAA Public Shares opted to redeem their shares for cash at a rate of US$10.86 per share, amounting to a total redemption value of US$222.827 million.

LTC, a holding company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operates through subsidiaries located in China and Europe. Prior to 2023, LTC's financial results included those of a former Variable Interest Entity (VIE). However, a significant restructuring occurred in 2023, wherein the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) acquired 100% equity in two subsidiaries of the former VIE. This restructuring involved the transfer of all assets and liabilities, excluding the Internet Content Provider (ICP) license, to a WFOE subsidiary, effectively terminating the VIE structure by June 30, 2023. LTC has indicated that this restructuring will not materially affect its operations or financial results.

The unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial statements reflect the Transactions as if they had occurred on December 31, 2023, for the balance sheet, and on January 1, 2022, for the statements of operations for the years ended December 31, 2023, and 2022. These statements have been prepared in accordance with SEC Regulation S-X, Article 11, and focus exclusively on Transaction Accounting Adjustments, deliberately excluding Management’s Adjustments. It is important to note that these financial statements are intended for illustrative purposes only and do not necessarily predict actual future results.

For a comprehensive understanding, the pro forma balance sheet and statements of operations should be read in conjunction with the audited financial statements of LCAA and LTC for the respective years ended December 31, 2023, and 2022, which are included in the prospectus.

Since early 2023, LTC has been implementing a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations, which is referred to as the “Restructuring.” In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the former VIE, and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into a series of agreements, or the VIE Restructuring Agreements, pursuant to which the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in the former VIE’s subsidiaries. title: 15,037,030 AMERICAN DEPOSITARY SHARES UNDERLYING WARRANTS, 680,957,495 AMERICAN DEPOSITARY SHARES AND 5,486,784 WARRANTS TO PURCHASE ORDINARY SHARES 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC) Securities Offering**

Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC), a Cayman Islands holding company, is offering up to 15,037,030 American depositary shares (ADSs), each representing one ordinary share with a par value of US$0.00001. This issuance includes:

1. **Public Warrants**: Up to 9,550,246 ADSs can be issued upon exercising public warrants at an exercise price of US$11.50, which were issued on February 22, 2024, in exchange for LCAA Public Warrants from L Catterton Asia.
   
2. **Sponsor Warrants**: Up to 5,486,784 ADSs can be issued upon exercising sponsor warrants at the same exercise price, issued to LCAA Acquisition Sponsor LLC in exchange for private placement warrants.

Additionally, the prospectus details the potential sale of 680,957,495 ADSs by selling securityholders, which includes:

- **Legacy Shares**: 542,850,129 ADSs owned by various entities, originally acquired at prices ranging from approximately US$0.6 to US$6.6 per share.
- **Sponsor Shares**: 7,162,718 ADSs issued to the Sponsor and independent directors at prices of US$0.003 and US$0.001 per share, respectively.
- **PIPE Shares**: 5,486,784 ADSs from sponsor warrants.
- **PIPE Investors**: 122,446,496 Ordinary Shares issued at US$10.00 per share.
- **CB Shares**: 3,011,368 Ordinary Shares issued at a conversion price of US$10.00 per share.

The registered securities encompass 680,957,495 Ordinary Shares, representing approximately 99.2% of total outstanding shares, and 5,486,784 Warrants, or about 36.5% of outstanding Warrants. The ADSs and Warrants are listed on Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW," respectively, with closing prices of US$6.38 and US$0.40 as of May 1, 2024.

The prospectus indicates that 20,518,519 LCAA Public Shares were redeemed for cash at US$10.86 per share, totaling US$222.8 million, which is approximately 94.2% of total LCAA Class A Ordinary Shares outstanding. The sale of these securities could significantly impact the market price of LTC's securities. While the company will not receive proceeds from sales by selling securityholders, it may receive proceeds from the exercise of Warrants if exercised for cash. The likelihood of warrant holders exercising their Warrants depends on the market price of the ADSs.

LTC qualifies as an "emerging growth company" under the JOBS Act and as a "foreign private issuer" under the Securities Exchange Act, allowing it to benefit from reduced reporting requirements. Additionally, Mr. Shufu Li holds over 50% of the voting power, classifying LTC as a "controlled company" under Nasdaq standards, which exempts it from certain corporate governance requirements.

LTC has restructured its operations, eliminating the variable interest entity (VIE) structure previously used. The restructuring involved the WFOE (Wuhan Lotus Technology Limited Company) acquiring 100% equity interest in the former VIE’s subsidiaries. Investors are acquiring interests in the holding company rather than in any operating subsidiaries, which presents unique risks. LTC relies on dividends from its subsidiaries for cash flow, which may be restricted by local laws, debt obligations, or regulatory actions from the People's Republic of China (PRC). The PRC government has significant authority over foreign investments and could impose regulations that adversely affect LTC’s operations and securities value.

As of now, LTC has not distributed any cash dividends to its shareholders, and the decision to declare dividends is at the discretion of the board of directors, subject to Cayman Islands law. The company has established cash management policies to ensure compliance with applicable laws, including anti-money laundering (AML) and know-your-customer (KYC) regulations.

Investing in LTC's securities carries a high degree of risk, particularly related to doing business in China, including regulatory approvals for overseas offerings and potential restrictions on foreign investments. Under the Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act (HFCAA), LTC’s securities could be prohibited from trading in the U.S. if the SEC determines that LTC has not filed audit reports from a PCAOB-registered firm that has been inspected for two consecutive years. Although the PCAOB has recently lifted restrictions on inspections in mainland China and Hong Kong, future determinations could still impact LTC’s compliance status.

**Key Figures:**
- **ADSs Underlying Warrants**: 15,037,030
- **Total ADSs Offered**: 680,957,495
- **Warrants to Purchase Ordinary Shares**: 5,486,784
- **No Cash Dividends Distributed to Date**

**Important Dates:**
- **Restructuring Initiated**: Early 2023
- **Annual Report on Form 20-F**: To be filed on April 22, 2024.

This summary encapsulates all critical information regarding LTC's securities offering, highlighting the structure, risks, and regulatory considerations pertinent to potential investors.

title: NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Notes to the Consolidated and Combined Financial Statements

The Company has encountered significant regulatory challenges concerning its Variable Interest Entities (VIEs), which may lead to potential fines for illegal operations, unmet compliance requirements, demands for restructuring that could affect ownership and control, restrictions on the use of overseas offering proceeds, and other adverse regulatory actions. Such penalties could jeopardize the Company's rights to direct VIE activities and receive economic benefits, ultimately preventing the consolidation of VIE financial results in its financial statements.

As a result of a restructuring, the Company ceased consolidating the VIEs effective June 30, 2023. This decision has notably impacted the Company's consolidated financial position, operations, and cash flows. Prior to the restructuring, the financial metrics for the VIEs as of December 31, 2022, were as follows:

- **Total Assets**: US$186,691, which included:
  - Cash: US$156,571
  - Inventories: US$108
  - Prepayments and other current assets: US$2,313
  - Property, equipment, and software (net): US$14,189
  - Operating lease right-of-use assets: US$12,388
  - Other non-current assets: US$1,122

- **Total Liabilities**: US$208,927, comprising:
  - Amounts due to inter-companies: US$10,551
  - Operating lease liabilities: US$716
  - Accrued expenses and other current liabilities (third parties): US$35,254
  - Accrued expenses and other current liabilities (related parties): US$801
  - Mandatorily redeemable noncontrolling interest: US$11,381
  - Additional operating lease liabilities: US$1,662
  - Exchangeable notes: US$71,792
  - Convertible notes: US$76,770

The financial data presented reflects the VIEs' position prior to the restructuring and underscores the complexities and risks associated with the VIE arrangements. Following the restructuring, all intercompany transactions and balances prior to this date have been eliminated upon consolidation. This situation highlights the critical need for the Company to navigate regulatory landscapes carefully to mitigate risks associated with its VIEs and ensure compliance moving forward.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Historically, Lotus Technology Inc. relied on contractual arrangements among Wuhan Lotus Technology Limited Company Ltd. (the “WFOE”), Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd., the former variable interest entity (“VIE”), and its nominee shareholders to direct the business operations of the former VIE and its subsidiaries. Since early 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has been implementing a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations (the “Restructuring”). In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the former VIE, and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into a series of agreements (the “VIE Restructuring Agreements”), pursuant to which the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in the former VIE’s subsidiaries. As of the date of this proxy statement/prospectus, Lotus Technology Inc.’s operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and Lotus Technology Inc. does not have any VIE structure. The holding company structure involves unique risks to investors. As a holding company, Lotus Technology Inc. may rely on dividends from its subsidiaries for cash requirements, including any payment of dividends to its shareholders. The ability of the subsidiaries of Lotus Technology Inc. to pay dividends or make distributions to Lotus Technology Inc. may be restricted by laws and regulations applicable to them or the debt they incur on their own behalf or the instruments governing their debt.

title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA) has received approval from its board of directors for a Merger Agreement involving Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC) and its subsidiaries, Lotus Temp Limited and Lotus EV Limited. The agreement, which outlines a two-step merger process, was initially dated January 31, 2023, and amended on October 11, 2023. In the first step, Lotus Temp Limited will merge with LCAA, making LCAA a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC, and converting its shareholders into LTC shareholders. The second step involves the surviving entity from the first merger merging into Lotus EV Limited, which will also become a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC. 

Shareholders of LCAA will vote on the proposed Business Combination, which, if approved, will transition their shares to LTC shares. Key actions prior to the merger include the conversion of LTC's preferred shares into ordinary shares, the adoption of amended articles of association, and a recapitalization process that will affect the share structure and outstanding options. 

LTC, a Cayman Islands holding company, has undergone a restructuring to eliminate its previous variable interest entity (VIE) structure, now directly operating through its subsidiaries in China. This change presents certain risks, including regulatory challenges in China and potential trading prohibitions in the U.S. under the Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act (HFCAA). The company plans to list its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) on Nasdaq following the merger.

The document also outlines the financial performance of LTC, including audited and unaudited financial statements, and details the registration fees associated with the securities being registered in connection with the merger. The extraordinary general meeting for LCAA shareholders will address the merger proposals, with the board recommending approval. However, shareholders are advised to consider potential conflicts of interest among LCAA's directors and officers regarding the Business Combination. The SEC has not evaluated the merits of the transactions, and any claims to the contrary are considered criminal offenses.

title: 15,037,030 AMERICAN DEPOSITARY SHARES UNDERLYING WARRANTS,  680,957,495 AMERICAN DEPOSITARY SHARES AND  5,486,784 WARRANTS TO PURCHASE ORDINARY SHARES 
summary: This prospectus details the issuance of up to 15,037,030 American depositary shares (ADSs) of Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC), each representing one ordinary share with a par value of US$0.00001. This includes 9,550,246 ADSs obtainable through public warrants issued on February 22, 2024, and 5,486,784 ADSs available via sponsor warrants, both at an exercise price of US$11.50 per share. The prospectus also discusses the potential sale of up to 680,957,495 ADSs by various selling securityholders, which encompasses legacy shares, sponsor shares, PIPE shares, and convertible note shares, as well as 5,486,784 warrants.

The registration of these securities aims to fulfill certain rights for selling securityholders, allowing them to sell at their discretion, potentially impacting the public trading price. The company will not receive proceeds from these sales but may benefit from warrant exercises if market conditions permit. LTC is classified as an "emerging growth company" and a "foreign private issuer," which provides it with certain regulatory advantages but also subjects it to specific risks, particularly related to its operations in mainland China. 

The restructuring of LTC has allowed it to operate directly through its subsidiaries, eliminating reliance on a variable interest entity structure. However, this holding company model presents risks, including regulatory constraints on dividend distributions and the complexities of doing business in China, which could adversely affect its operations and securities value. 

Furthermore, under the Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act (HFCAA), LTC's securities could face trading bans if audit reports are not from PCAOB-inspected firms. While recent developments have allowed for PCAOB inspections in China, any future loss of access could lead to significant trading restrictions. 

Investing in LTC carries substantial risks, and the prospectus emphasizes that the SEC has not approved or verified the securities mentioned, warning against any misleading claims regarding regulatory endorsements.

title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA) has received approval from its board of directors for a Merger Agreement with Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC) and its subsidiaries, which involves a two-step merger process. Initially, LCAA will merge with Lotus Temp Limited, becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC, and subsequently, the surviving entity will merge with Lotus EV Limited. This series of transactions, referred to as the Business Combination, was first dated January 31, 2023, and amended on October 11, 2023. Shareholders of LCAA are set to vote on this agreement and related proposals at an extraordinary general meeting scheduled for February 2, 2024.

On the Closing Date, several actions will take place, including the conversion of LTC's preferred shares into ordinary shares and the adoption of amended articles of association. A significant recapitalization will occur, adjusting the number of LTC Ordinary Shares and options, with specific provisions for rounding down fractional shares. The agreement also details the conversion and exchange of LCAA shares and warrants, including the cancellation of Class B shares and the separation of units into Class A shares and warrants.

Post-merger, ownership of LTC Ordinary Shares is projected to be distributed among existing LTC shareholders, LCAA public shareholders, and LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP, with Mr. Shufu Li expected to hold over 50% of voting power, potentially designating LTC as a "controlled company." As a foreign private issuer based in the Cayman Islands, LTC will follow local corporate governance practices, which may exempt it from certain Nasdaq rules.

The tax implications of the merger are uncertain, hinging on whether it qualifies as a tax-free reorganization under U.S. tax law. LTC's operations, primarily in mainland China, face regulatory risks that could impact its financial health and ability to distribute dividends, as well as potential trading prohibitions under the Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act if its auditor remains uninspectable.

LTC plans to list its American depositary shares (ADSs) and warrants on Nasdaq, contingent on the successful completion of the Business Combination. The document detailing these transactions includes comprehensive financial statements and risk factors, urging shareholders to review it thoroughly before voting. The board of LCAA recommends approval of the merger, while also highlighting potential conflicts of interest among directors and officers.
